name: Lemon Protein Cookies
description: Lemon flavor of the Crumbl base — base chassis + lemon juice in the dough, and the base's frosting sub_recipe pin dropped in favor of a lemon drizzle (lemon juice + granulated Splenda; never monk fruit). Derived off Crumbl Base Protein Cookie. NOTE: the drizzle was rated 'bad' on the original (too weak/thin) and granulated Splenda won't glaze as smoothly as a powdered sweetener — still a redesign candidate. Untested at this base chassis.

payload.instructionText: Cool the cookies fully. Whisk the drizzle (lemon juice + Splenda) smooth and spoon over the cookies.

payload.text: 9 min at 350F is the hard ceiling — these cookies will look underdone in the center and that's correct. Protein cookies firm dramatically as they cool; pulling at visual 'done' gives you hockey pucks.

The 20-30 min chill is load-bearing: it firms the butter and hydrates the cornstarch so the thick dough holds its scooped mound and spreads slowly, giving thick centers. Skip it and you get flat, greasy discs.

Granulated Splenda won't dissolve smoothly in lemon juice the way powdered sweetener does — the drizzle will be gritty unless you whisk it vigorously for 30+ sec and apply immediately before Splenda re-crystallizes.
The 15 g lemon juice goes in with the wet ingredients at b2; add it after the butter so the acid doesn't break the emulsion before everything is combined. Mix to a shaggy dough, not a smooth paste.
The drizzle is flagged 'needs-tuning' — granulated Splenda is the weak link. A swap to powdered Splenda (same volume) makes a noticeably smoother glaze that clings instead of beading.
